title: 'Life is meaningless without a pursuit' — Oct 2019 success-is-rented thesis
html:
TL;DR

  • Three-line manifesto note from Oct 16, 2019. Title is the meaning-of-life claim — "Life is meaningless without a pursuit" — body is the supporting trio: "It doesn't matter what happened to you, what matters is what you do about it" / "Attitude × Skill × Effort = Performance" / "Success is rented every day and not owned, once you believe it's owned you begin to fall."
  • Genesis source for the success is rented concept (which already cites this note and the Nov 9 follow-up 2019 11 09 it doesent matter what happens to you it matter how you reac). Three months before Yahir turns 17 he has fully articulated the multiplicative performance equation and the rented-not-owned success model — the operating-philosophy load-bearing for everything he later does.
  • The multiplicative framing is the load-bearing systems-thinking move: any zero zeros the product. This is the same instinct that produced the Pokémon type effectiveness chart note nine months earlier (2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything) — matrix logic applied to identity/performance.
  • Title-body-thesis triple structure — title states the claim, body proves the claim from three angles (resilience, decomposition, durability). This is the densest aphorism architecture in the 2019 corpus, fully manifesto-form.
  • Likely sourced (in part) from John Maxwell — the "Attitude × Skill × Effort" equation has the Maxwell fingerprint and Yahir was actively reading him in this stretch (referenced in 2019 08 12 keep things simple). Same with "success is rented" — common motivational-coaching line, but Yahir is operationalizing it, not just quoting it.

Key quotes

"It doesn't matter what happened to you / What matters is what you do about it"

"Attitude × Skill × Effort = Performance"

"Success is rented every day and not owned, once you believe it's owned you begin to fall"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The most compressed teen-thesis in the corpus. Three ideas in three lines, each of which becomes a recurring axiom across the next 7 years. Nothing else in 2018-2019 carries this much load per byte.
  • The rented-not-owned durability rule is what makes the 2024-2025 willingness to walk from Brotein / BroMenu toward Rankett make sense — Yahir doesn't own the previous wins, so abandoning them costs less. Pair against 2018 12 01 yall if you are not obsessed with your goals choose new ones (Dec 2018 obsession-line) — the two together justify the pivoting.
  • Companion piece to 2019 11 09 it doesent matter what happens to you it matter how you reac (Nov 2019), which restates line 1 verbatim and adds a DaBaby attribution — the Oct→Nov pair shows Yahir was iterating on the thesis, not just one-off jotting it.

Open questions

  • Specific source for the "Attitude × Skill × Effort" equation? Maxwell, Tony Robbins, or original?
  • Is there a 2020+ note where the multiplicative framing is applied to a specific decision (e.g., deciding which scholarship to chase)?
  • Does the "once you believe it's owned" warning ever land on a specific moment of complacency Yahir caught himself in?

Wikilinks

success is rented · fear of not doing · disconnected from outcome · grants pass teen era · systems thinking · type effectiveness chart · john maxwell
